result.stdout:
peer 1.10.0
Copyright (C) 2026 wareismymind
Shows the details of a specific pull request including description and check
info

  --help          Display this help screen.

  --version       Display version information.

  value pos. 0    Required. A full or partial pull request identifier of the
                  form provider/org/repo/id partials can be built up from
                  combinations of sections. For example: '6' to get pull the
                  pull request with id 6 provided there is only one. If there
                  are more than one match you'll have to disambiguate by adding
                  additional qualifiers eg. repoName/6


Since Checks have both a completion state and a result the mapping between
states and symbols in the details command are shown below:

Status: InProgress Result: Unknown => 🔵
Status: Completed  Result: Success => 🟢
Status: Completed  Result: Failure => 🔴
Status: Completed  Result: Neutral => 🤷
Status: Completed  Result: Skipped => 🪢
Status: Completed  Result: Timeout => 🐢

result.stdout:
peer 1.10.0
Copyright (C) 2026 wareismymind
Shows a list of your pull requests and their statuses with applied transforms

  -s, --sort      The sort key and direction you would like to use. The default
                  sort direction is ascending.
                  <key>:[(asc|ascending)|(desc|descending)]

  -w, --watch     

  -c, --count     (Default: 40)

  -f, --filter    

  --help          Display this help screen.

  --version       Display version information.


-- SYMBOLS --

The symbols in the show command and their meanings are shown below:

  Unknown        => ❔
  Draft          => 📃
  Conflict       => ⚔️
  FailedChecks   => 🔥
  ActionsQueued  => 💤
  ActionsPending => ⌛
  AwaitingReview => 🚩
  FixesRequested => ❌
  ReadyToMerge   => ✔️
  Merged         => 🎊
  Stale          => 🍞

-- SORTING --

The sort option can use a number of different keys and default to ascending
order. The avaliable keys are:

  repo
  id-lex
  id
  owner
  author
  status
  active
  title

-- FILTERING --

Filtering can be done on any of the sort keys. The filter syntax is:
'key:value', negation is provided by prepending a '!' before the filter key.

  - Numbers can be matched only for equality (or inequality)
  - Strings will be matched by regex
  - The pull request status filter is an enum based filter and supports all of
  the statuses from the previous section.

The filter key types are listed below:

  repo   => String
  id-lex => String
  id     => Int32
  owner  => String
  author => String
  status => PullRequestStatus
  active => Int32
  title  => String

Copyright (C) 2026 wareismymind
Shows a list of your pull requests and their statuses with applied transforms

  -s, --sort      The sort key and direction you would like to use. The default
                  sort direction is ascending.
                  <key>:[(asc|ascending)|(desc|descending)]

  -w, --watch     

  -c, --count     (Default: 40)

  -f, --filter    

  --help          Display this help screen.

  --version       Display version information.


-- SYMBOLS --

The symbols in the show command and their meanings are shown below:

  Unknown        => ❔
  Draft          => 📃
  Conflict       => ⚔️
  FailedChecks   => 🔥
  ActionsQueued  => 💤
  ActionsPending => ⌛
  AwaitingReview => 🚩
  FixesRequested => ❌
  ReadyToMerge   => ✔️
  Merged         => 🎊
  Stale          => 🍞

-- SORTING --

The sort option can use a number of different keys and default to ascending
order. The avaliable keys are:

  repo
  id-lex
  id
  owner
  author
  status
  active
  title

-- FILTERING --

Filtering can be done on any of the sort keys. The filter syntax is:
'key:value', negation is provided by prepending a '!' before the filter key.

  - Numbers can be matched only for equality (or inequality)
  - Strings will be matched by regex
  - The pull request status filter is an enum based filter and supports all of
  the statuses from the previous section.

The filter key types are listed below:

  repo   => String
  id-lex => String
  id     => Int32
  owner  => String
  author => String
  status => PullRequestStatus
  active => Int32
  title  => String
